section_name: Authority over Sidewalk in Home-rule Municipality
chapter_title: Gen. Provisions Relating to Mun. Streets
text:
A home-rule municipality may:

(1) construct a sidewalk;

(2) provide for the improvement of a sidewalk or the construction of a curb under an ordinance enforced by a penal provision; or

(3) declare a defective sidewalk to be a public nuisance.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 6
### Chapter 311. Gen. Provisions Relating to Mun. Streets
##### § 311.004 — Authority over Sidewalk in Home-rule Municipality

A home-rule municipality may:

    (1) construct a sidewalk;

    (2) provide for the improvement of a sidewalk or the construction of a curb under an ordinance enforced by a penal provision; or

    (3) declare a defective sidewalk to be a public nuisance.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.text: provide for the improvement of a sidewalk or the construction of a curb under an ordinance enforced by a penal provision; or
declare a defective sidewalk to be a public nuisance.
